channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
item.description: Officials in Russia-occupied Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales Sunday as Ukraine ramped up attacks on fuel supplies on the Black Sea peninsula.
item.content:encoded:
Officials in Russia-occupied Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales Sunday as Ukraine ramped up attacks on fuel supplies on the Black Sea peninsula.

(Image credit: Andriy Andriyenko/Ukrainian 65 Mechanized brigade)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump claims vandals damaged D.C. Reflecting Pool, and says it will be drained again
item.description: The president posted on Truth Social claiming vandals slashed the pool's lining and poured chemicals into the water, saying arrests have been made. He provided no evidence for his claims.
item.content:encoded:
The president posted on Truth Social claiming vandals slashed the pool's lining and poured chemicals into the water, saying arrests have been made. He provided no evidence for his claims.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: A new survey on dads found that 9 out of 10 had a surprising reaction to fatherhood
item.description: Men are traditionally thought of as providers for their children. But a report that interviewed thousands of fathers found them embracing another role.
item.content:encoded:
Men are traditionally thought of as providers for their children. But a report that interviewed thousands of fathers found them embracing another role.

(Image credit: From left: family photo; family photo; family photo)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan

item.title: Freed from Cambodia's scam compounds, trafficking victims face a new crisis
item.description: Cambodia's crackdown on scam centers has created a secondary crisis: thousands of stranded foreign workers are now roaming the streets of Phnom Penh.
item.content:encoded: Cambodia's crackdown on scam centers has created a secondary crisis: thousands of stranded foreign workers are now roaming the streets of Phnom Penh.
item.dc:creator: Shibani Mahtani

item.title: Want to improve your agility? Try these 'explosive power' exercises
item.description: These simple movements combine speed and strength to train your body's ability to rapidly generate force. They can also help prevent injury and boost agility.
item.content:encoded:
These simple movements combine speed and strength to train your body's ability to rapidly generate force. They can also help prevent injury and boost agility.

(Image credit: Margaret Cirino)
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: DOJ memo stokes fear among disability advocates of a return to institutionalization
item.description: The Justice Department's opinion challenges civil rights protections that have long treated the institutionalization of disabled Americans as a last resort.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department's opinion challenges civil rights protections that have long treated the institutionalization of disabled Americans as a last resort.

(Image credit: Patrick Semansky)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Opinion: Algae doesn't care about our party lines
item.description: President Trump's beautification project of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has become plagued with a robust algae bloom, despite a $14 million investment and a coating of "American flag blue."
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's beautification project of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has become plagued with a robust algae bloom, despite a $14 million investment and a coating of "American flag blue."

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: These nuns spent a lifetime helping others. In their last years, who will help them?
item.description: The sisters of Uganda are teachers, health-care advocates and more. Those who are in their twilight of their life need help. Who will come to their aid?
item.content:encoded:
The sisters of Uganda are teachers, health-care advocates and more. Those who are in their twilight of their life need help. Who will come to their aid?

(Image credit: Stuart Tibaweswa for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sophie Neiman

item.title: JD Vance arrives in Switzerland for talks with Iran over fragile peace deal
item.description: JD Vance arrived in Switzerland on Sunday for talks with Iranian officials, hoping to consolidate the fragile interim deal to end the war and kickstart fresh discussions over Tehran's nuclear program.
item.content:encoded:
JD Vance arrived in Switzerland on Sunday for talks with Iranian officials, hoping to consolidate the fragile interim deal to end the war and kickstart fresh discussions over Tehran's nuclear program.

(Image credit: Hassan Ammar)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Palestinian-American kids find inspiration, and escape, on the soccer pitch
item.description: A dozen miles away from the World Cup games in New York/ New Jersey Stadium, Palestinian-American kids turn to soccer as an escape from the realities of war.
item.content:encoded:
A dozen miles away from the World Cup games in New York/ New Jersey Stadium, Palestinian-American kids turn to soccer as an escape from the realities of war.

(Image credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd
